en_definition: a laboratory method used to find the concentration of a solution
example1: We used titration to determine the acid concentration.
example1_ru: Мы использовали титрование, чтобы определить концентрацию кислоты.
example2: The titration must be stopped exactly at the color change.

en_definition: a laboratory tool used to measure and transfer small amounts of liquid
example1: Use a pipette to add exactly two milliliters of solution.
example1_ru: Используйте пипетку, чтобы добавить ровно два миллилитра раствора.
example2: Rinse the pipette between each measurement.

en_definition: a graduated glass tube used to dispense precise volumes of liquid
example1: Fill the burette carefully to avoid air bubbles.
example1_ru: Аккуратно заполните бюретку, чтобы избежать пузырьков воздуха.
example2: Read the burette scale at eye level for accuracy.

en_definition: a glass container with a narrow neck used in laboratories
example1: Pour the solution into a clean flask before heating.
example1_ru: Перелейте раствор в чистую колбу перед нагреванием.
example2: The flask cracked when it was heated too quickly.

en_definition: a tool with a wide top and narrow bottom used to pour liquids
example1: Use a funnel to transfer the powder into the bottle.
example1_ru: Используйте воронку, чтобы пересыпать порошок в бутылку.
example2: The funnel helps avoid spills during filtration.

en_definition: the process of separating solids from liquids using a filter
example1: Filtration removes solid particles from the mixture before further analysis.
example1_ru: Фильтрация удаляет твёрдые частицы из смеси перед дальнейшим анализом.
example2: After filtration, the liquid should be completely clear.

en_definition: not able to be dissolved in a liquid
example1: The powder proved insoluble in cold water even after stirring.
example1_ru: Порошок оказался нерастворимым в холодной воде даже после перемешивания.
example2: Insoluble particles settled at the bottom of the beaker.

en_definition: a solution that cannot dissolve any more of a substance at a given temperature
example1: No more sugar will dissolve once the solution becomes saturated.
example1_ru: Как только раствор становится насыщенным, сахар больше не растворяется.
example2: Heat the saturated solution to dissolve additional crystals.

en_definition: the process by which a liquid changes into a gas
example1: Evaporation increases sharply as the temperature of the liquid rises.
example1_ru: Испарение резко усиливается по мере роста температуры жидкости.
example2: The salt was recovered from the water through evaporation.

en_definition: the process by which a gas turns into a liquid
example1: Condensation formed on the cold glass surface within a few minutes.
example1_ru: На холодной стеклянной поверхности за несколько минут образовалась конденсация.
example2: The device collects fresh water through condensation.

en_definition: the process by which a solid changes directly into a gas
example1: Dry ice undergoes sublimation at room temperature without becoming liquid.
example1_ru: Сухой лёд при комнатной температуре подвергается сублимации, минуя жидкое состояние.
example2: Sublimation is used to purify certain solid chemicals.

en_definition: a material that allows electricity or heat to pass through it easily
example1: Copper is an excellent conductor of electricity used in most wiring.
example1_ru: Медь — отличный проводник электричества, используемый в большинстве проводок.
example2: Metals are generally better conductors than plastics.

en_definition: a material that does not conduct electricity or heat well
example1: Rubber is used as an insulator around electrical wires.
example1_ru: Резину используют как изолятор вокруг электрических проводов.
example2: Wood is a poor conductor and acts as an insulator.

en_definition: a material with electrical conductivity between a conductor and an insulator
example1: Silicon is the most common semiconductor used in electronics.
example1_ru: Кремний — самый распространённый полупроводник в электронике.
example2: Semiconductors are essential components of modern computer chips.

en_definition: an electrical component that limits the flow of current
example1: Add a resistor to the circuit to control the current.
example1_ru: Добавьте резистор в цепь, чтобы контролировать ток.
example2: The resistor prevents the bulb from burning out.

en_definition: a device that stores electrical energy in a circuit
example1: The capacitor stores charge and releases it when needed.
example1_ru: Конденсатор накапливает заряд и высвобождает его по необходимости.
example2: A faulty capacitor can cause the device to malfunction.

en_definition: a metal made by combining two or more metallic elements
example1: Steel is an alloy of iron and carbon.
example1_ru: Сталь — это сплав железа и углерода.
example2: Engineers chose a lightweight alloy for the aircraft frame.

en_definition: the gradual destruction of a material, especially metal, caused by chemical reactions
example1: Corrosion weakened the metal supports over the years.
example1_ru: Коррозия со временем ослабила металлические опоры.
example2: A protective coating helps prevent corrosion.

en_definition: easily bent or shaped without breaking
example1: The heated plastic becomes pliable and easy to mold.
example1_ru: Нагретый пластик становится гибким, и его легко формовать.
example2: Young branches are more pliable than old, dry ones.

en_definition: able to be stretched into a thin wire without breaking
example1: Copper is highly ductile, which makes it useful for wiring.
example1_ru: Медь очень пластична, поэтому её используют для проводки.
example2: Gold is one of the most ductile metals known.

en_definition: able to be hammered or pressed into different shapes without breaking
example1: Aluminum is light and malleable, so it is easy to shape.
example1_ru: Алюминий лёгкий и ковкий, поэтому его легко формовать.
example2: The metal remains malleable at high temperatures.

en_definition: having many small holes that allow liquid or air to pass through
example1: The porous rock absorbed water quickly during the storm.
example1_ru: Пористая порода быстро впитывала воду во время бури.
example2: Porous materials are often used in filtration systems.

en_definition: not allowing liquid or gas to pass through
example1: The roof is covered with an impermeable layer to stop leaks.
example1_ru: Крыша покрыта непроницаемым слоем, чтобы предотвратить протечки.
example2: Clay soil is nearly impermeable to water.

en_definition: a material made from two or more different substances combined together
example1: Carbon fiber is a composite material used in aircraft design.
example1_ru: Углеродное волокно — это композитный материал, используемый в авиастроении.
example2: Composite materials are often stronger and lighter than pure metals.

en_definition: the amount of heat needed to raise the temperature of a substance by one degree
example1: Water has a high specific heat compared to most metals.
example1_ru: У воды высокая удельная теплоёмкость по сравнению с большинством металлов.
example2: Calculate the specific heat required to warm the sample.

en_definition: the ability of a material to conduct heat
example1: Metals typically have high thermal conductivity compared to plastics or wood.
example1_ru: Металлы обычно обладают высокой теплопроводностью по сравнению с пластиком или деревом.
example2: Insulation materials have low thermal conductivity.

en_definition: the energy that a moving object has
example1: The ball's kinetic energy increases as it rolls faster.
example1_ru: Кинетическая энергия мяча возрастает по мере увеличения скорости.
example2: Kinetic energy is transferred during a collision.

en_definition: the stored energy an object has because of its position or state
example1: The object gains potential energy as it is lifted higher.
example1_ru: Объект накапливает потенциальную энергию по мере подъёма выше.
example2: Potential energy converts to kinetic energy as the object falls.

en_definition: the principle that energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed
example1: The conservation of energy explains why the pendulum eventually slows.
example1_ru: Закон сохранения энергии объясняет, почему маятник в итоге замедляется.
example2: This experiment demonstrates the conservation of energy in a closed system.

en_definition: a number representing the friction between two surfaces
example1: A higher friction coefficient means more resistance to sliding.
example1_ru: Более высокий коэффициент трения означает большее сопротивление скольжению.
example2: The lab measured the friction coefficient of different materials.

en_definition: the maximum constant speed reached by a falling object
example1: A skydiver reaches terminal velocity after a few seconds of free fall.
example1_ru: Парашютист достигает предельной скорости падения через несколько секунд свободного падения.
example2: Air resistance determines the terminal velocity of a falling object.

en_definition: the change in position of an object in a particular direction
example1: The displacement of the object was measured in meters.
example1_ru: Смещение объекта было измерено в метрах.
example2: Displacement differs from distance because it includes direction.

en_definition: the ability of an object to float in a liquid or gas
example1: The boat's design gives it enough buoyancy to stay afloat.
example1_ru: Конструкция лодки обеспечивает достаточную плавучесть, чтобы держаться на воде.
example2: Buoyancy depends on the density of both the object and the fluid.

en_definition: the bending of light as it passes from one substance to another
example1: Refraction causes a straw to look bent in a glass of water.
example1_ru: Преломление света делает так, что соломинка кажется изогнутой в стакане воды.
example2: The lens works by using the refraction of light.

en_definition: the bouncing back of light, sound, or heat from a surface
example1: The mirror uses reflection to show a clear image.
example1_ru: Зеркало использует отражение, чтобы показать чёткое изображение.
example2: Sound reflection causes an echo in large empty rooms.

en_definition: the spreading out of waves as they pass through a gap or around an obstacle
example1: Diffraction explains why sound can be heard around corners.
example1_ru: Дифракция объясняет, почему звук слышен из-за угла.
example2: The experiment demonstrated the diffraction of light through a narrow slit.

en_definition: the process by which light waves are made to vibrate in a single direction
example1: Polarized sunglasses reduce glare from bright surfaces through polarization.
example1_ru: Поляризационные солнцезащитные очки уменьшают блики от ярких поверхностей благодаря поляризации.
example2: Polarization is used in many optical instruments.

en_definition: the study of light broken into its component wavelengths to identify substances
example1: Spectrum analysis revealed the chemical makeup of the star.
example1_ru: Спектральный анализ выявил химический состав звезды.
example2: Astronomers rely on spectrum analysis to study distant galaxies.

en_definition: a form of an element with the same number of protons but a different number of neutrons
example1: Carbon-14 is a radioactive isotope used in dating old objects.
example1_ru: Углерод-14 — радиоактивный изотоп, используемый для датировки древних предметов.
example2: Some isotopes are stable, while others decay over time.

en_definition: the process by which an unstable atom loses energy over time
example1: Radioactive decay releases particles and energy from the nucleus.
example1_ru: Радиоактивный распад высвобождает частицы и энергию из ядра атома.
example2: Scientists measure radioactive decay to determine the age of fossils.

en_definition: the time it takes for half of a radioactive substance to decay
example1: The half-life of this isotope is about five thousand years.
example1_ru: Период полураспада этого изотопа составляет около пяти тысяч лет.
example2: Knowing the half-life helps estimate the age of a sample.

en_definition: the complete set of genetic material in an organism
example1: Scientists mapped the entire human genome by 2003.
example1_ru: К 2003 году учёные полностью расшифровали геном человека.
example2: Comparing genomes helps researchers study evolution.

en_definition: a change in the genetic material of an organism
example1: The mutation caused a noticeable change in eye color.
example1_ru: Мутация вызвала заметное изменение цвета глаз.
example2: Some mutations have no visible effect on the organism.

en_definition: a protein that speeds up chemical reactions in living organisms
example1: This enzyme breaks down starch into simple sugars.
example1_ru: Этот фермент расщепляет крахмал на простые сахара.
example2: Enzymes work best within a specific temperature range.

en_definition: the process by which plants use sunlight to produce food
example1: Photosynthesis converts carbon dioxide and water into glucose and oxygen.
example1_ru: Фотосинтез превращает углекислый газ и воду в глюкозу и кислород.
example2: Plants need sunlight for photosynthesis to occur.

en_definition: the process by which living cells release energy from food
example1: Cellular respiration releases the energy stored in glucose.
example1_ru: Клеточное дыхание высвобождает энергию, хранящуюся в глюкозе.
example2: Respiration requires oxygen and produces carbon dioxide.

en_definition: the variety of plant and animal species in an environment
example1: Rainforests contain some of the highest biodiversity on Earth.
example1_ru: Тропические леса обладают одним из самых высоких показателей биоразнообразия на Земле.
example2: Pollution can seriously reduce local biodiversity.

en_definition: a series of organisms that depend on each other for food
example1: Removing one species can disrupt the entire food chain.
example1_ru: Устранение одного вида может нарушить всю пищевую цепь.
example2: Energy flows from one level of the food chain to the next.

en_definition: the complete disappearance of a species from existence
example1: Habitat loss is a major cause of species extinction.
example1_ru: Утрата среды обитания — главная причина вымирания видов.
example2: Conservation programs try to prevent extinction of endangered animals.

en_definition: the trapping of heat in the atmosphere by certain gases
example1: The greenhouse effect keeps the planet warm enough to support life.
example1_ru: Парниковый эффект поддерживает достаточно тёплую температуру планеты для жизни.
example2: Excess carbon dioxide intensifies the greenhouse effect.

en_definition: a substance released into the air, especially a pollutant
example1: Factories must reduce their carbon emissions by law.
example1_ru: По закону заводы обязаны сокращать выбросы углерода.
example2: Vehicle emissions contribute significantly to air pollution.

en_definition: the ability to be maintained without depleting resources or causing harm
example1: The course focuses on sustainability in modern engineering.
example1_ru: Курс посвящён устойчивости в современной инженерии.
example2: Sustainability requires balancing economic and environmental needs.

en_definition: producing no net release of carbon dioxide into the atmosphere
example1: The university aims to become carbon neutral by 2030.
example1_ru: Университет намерен стать углеродно нейтральным к 2030 году.
example2: Carbon neutral products offset their emissions elsewhere.

en_definition: a simplified description of a program's steps, not written in a real programming language
example1: Write pseudocode before translating your idea into real code.
example1_ru: Напишите псевдокод, прежде чем переводить идею в настоящий код.
example2: The textbook explains each algorithm using pseudocode.

en_definition: one repetition of a process, especially in programming
example1: Each iteration of the loop adds one to the total.
example1_ru: Каждая итерация цикла увеличивает сумму на единицу.
example2: The design improved with every iteration of the prototype.

en_definition: the set of rules that define how code or a language must be structured
example1: A single syntax error can prevent the whole program from running.
example1_ru: Одна синтаксическая ошибка может помешать запуску всей программы.
example2: Learn the basic syntax before writing complex programs.

en_definition: to convert source code into a form the computer can run
example1: The program failed to compile because of a missing bracket.
example1_ru: Программа не скомпилировалась из-за пропущенной скобки.
example2: Compile the code and check for any error messages.

en_definition: to run a program or carry out a command
example1: Click the button to execute the program and check the output.
example1_ru: Нажмите кнопку, чтобы выполнить программу и проверить результат.
example2: The instructions were executed in the wrong order.

en_definition: the process of finding and fixing errors in a program
example1: Debugging took longer than writing the original code.
example1_ru: Отладка заняла больше времени, чем написание самого кода.
example2: Use a debugger to make debugging easier.

en_definition: the process of converting information into a secret code
example1: Encryption protects sensitive data from being read by others.
example1_ru: Шифрование защищает конфиденциальные данные от прочтения посторонними.
example2: The website uses strong encryption for online payments.

en_definition: the delay before data begins to transfer over a network
example1: High latency makes online gaming and video calls frustrating for users.
example1_ru: Высокая задержка сети делает игру в сети и видеозвонки неудобными для пользователей.
example2: The new connection reduced latency significantly.

en_definition: a period during which a server is not working or available
example1: Server downtime prevented students from submitting their assignments.
example1_ru: Из-за простоя сервера студенты не смогли сдать задания.
example2: The university scheduled server downtime for maintenance overnight.

en_definition: the part of software that a person interacts with directly
example1: The new app has a much simpler user interface.
example1_ru: У нового приложения намного более простой пользовательский интерфейс.
example2: A confusing user interface frustrates new users.

en_definition: the delivery of computing services over the internet
example1: Cloud computing allows students to access files from any device.
example1_ru: Облачные вычисления позволяют студентам получать доступ к файлам с любого устройства.
example2: Many companies rely on cloud computing to store large datasets.

en_definition: the ability of a computer system to perform tasks that normally require human intelligence
example1: Artificial intelligence is transforming many industries, including education.
example1_ru: Искусственный интеллект меняет многие отрасли, включая образование.
example2: The course introduces the basic principles of artificial intelligence.

en_definition: a type of artificial intelligence in which computers learn from data
example1: Machine learning models improve as they process more data.
example1_ru: Модели машинного обучения улучшаются по мере обработки большего объёма данных.
example2: The project uses machine learning to predict student performance.

en_definition: an incident in which private information is accessed without permission
example1: The university reported a data breach affecting student records.
example1_ru: Университет сообщил об утечке данных, затронувшей студенческие записи.
example2: Strong passwords help prevent data breaches.

en_definition: the process of proving that a person or device is who or what it claims to be
example1: Two-factor authentication adds an extra layer of security.
example1_ru: Двухфакторная аутентификация добавляет дополнительный уровень безопасности.
example2: Authentication failed because the password was entered incorrectly.

en_definition: software designed to damage or gain unauthorized access to a computer
example1: The antivirus program detected malware on the laptop.
example1_ru: Антивирусная программа обнаружила вредоносное ПО на ноутбуке.
example2: Avoid downloading files from unknown sources to prevent malware.

en_definition: an alternative source of electricity used when the main power fails
example1: The lab has a backup power supply in case of an outage.
example1_ru: В лаборатории есть резервный источник питания на случай отключения электричества.
example2: Servers rely on a backup power supply to avoid data loss.

en_definition: the mental process of acquiring knowledge and understanding
example1: The lecture explored how memory affects human cognition.
example1_ru: Лекция была посвящена тому, как память влияет на человеческое мышление.
example2: Sleep plays an important role in cognition and learning.

en_definition: the way something is understood or interpreted through the senses
example1: Color perception can vary slightly between individuals depending on their eyesight.
example1_ru: Восприятие цвета может немного отличаться у разных людей в зависимости от зрения.
example2: The experiment tested visual perception under low light.

en_definition: the process of thinking about something logically to reach a conclusion
example1: Show your reasoning clearly in the answer to this problem.
example1_ru: Чётко покажите ход своих рассуждений в ответе на эту задачу.
example2: Her reasoning was sound, even if the conclusion was wrong.

en_definition: the ability to understand something immediately without conscious reasoning
example1: Her intuition told her that the experiment would fail.
example1_ru: Интуиция подсказывала ей, что эксперимент провалится.
example2: Trust your intuition, but always double-check with evidence.

en_definition: to remember facts or details over time
example1: Students retain information better when they review it regularly.
example1_ru: Студенты лучше запоминают информацию, если регулярно её повторяют.
example2: Repetition helps the brain retain information long-term.

en_definition: the amount of mental effort being used in working memory
example1: Too much text on a slide increases cognitive load.
example1_ru: Слишком много текста на слайде увеличивает когнитивную нагрузку.
example2: Breaking a task into steps reduces cognitive load.

en_definition: the ability to analyze facts and form a judgment
example1: The course aims to develop students' critical thinking skills.
example1_ru: Курс направлен на развитие у студентов навыков критического мышления.
example2: Critical thinking helps you evaluate sources before citing them.

en_definition: the process of finding solutions to difficult or complex problems
example1: Group projects help improve teamwork and problem-solving among classmates.
example1_ru: Групповые проекты помогают развивать командную работу и навыки решения проблем среди одногруппников.
example2: This module focuses on practical problem-solving techniques.

en_definition: to bring a memory or fact back into your mind
example1: Try to recall the exact wording of the question.
example1_ru: Постарайтесь вспомнить точную формулировку вопроса.
example2: She could not recall where she had put her notes.

en_definition: the mental system used to temporarily hold and process information
example1: Working memory allows you to solve a math problem step by step.
example1_ru: Оперативная память позволяет решать математическую задачу шаг за шагом.
example2: Multitasking places a heavy load on working memory.

en_definition: to formally inform the academic department about something
example1: You must notify the department if you plan to withdraw.
example1_ru: Вы обязаны уведомить кафедру, если планируете отчислиться.
example2: Notify the department immediately in case of illness before an exam.

en_definition: to formally send in a written request for something
example1: Submit a request for a transcript through the online portal.
example1_ru: Подайте заявку на получение выписки через онлайн-портал.
example2: You can submit a request to change your course before the deadline.

en_definition: to arrange a specific time to meet someone
example1: Book an appointment with your advisor before choosing electives.
example1_ru: Запишитесь на приём к своему куратору перед выбором факультативов.
example2: I need to book an appointment at the health center.

en_definition: to extend the period of belonging to a club or service
example1: Renew your gym membership before it expires next week.
example1_ru: Продлите членство в спортзале до истечения срока на следующей неделе.
example2: You can renew your library membership online.

en_definition: to officially make a complaint
example1: Students can lodge a complaint through the student services office.
example1_ru: Студенты могут подать жалобу через службу поддержки студентов.
example2: He lodged a complaint about the noise in the dormitory.

en_definition: money paid back to someone who has spent their own money
example1: Keep your receipts to claim reimbursement for travel expenses.
example1_ru: Сохраняйте чеки, чтобы получить возмещение транспортных расходов.
example2: Reimbursement usually takes about two weeks to process.

en_definition: a formal written request signed by many people or submitted to authorities
example1: Students signed a petition asking for extended library hours.
example1_ru: Студенты подписали петицию с просьбой продлить часы работы библиотеки.
example2: The petition was submitted to the student council.

en_definition: the minimum number of people needed to make a meeting official
example1: The meeting could not begin because there was no quorum.
example1_ru: Собрание не могло начаться из-за отсутствия кворума.
example2: A quorum of ten members is required to vote on the proposal.

en_definition: a topic listed for discussion at a meeting
example1: The budget was the first agenda item at the meeting.
example1_ru: Бюджет был первым пунктом повестки дня на собрании.
example2: Please add your proposal as a new agenda item.

en_definition: an official written record of what was discussed and decided at a meeting
example1: The secretary took the minutes of the meeting.
example1_ru: Секретарь вёл протокол собрания.
example2: The minutes of the meeting were shared with all members afterward.

en_definition: agreed by everyone without any objections
example1: The committee reached a unanimous decision on the new policy.
example1_ru: Комитет принял единогласное решение по новой политике.
example2: The proposal was approved by a unanimous vote.

en_definition: to choose not to vote either for or against something
example1: Two members chose to abstain from voting on the issue.
example1_ru: Двое членов решили воздержаться от голосования по этому вопросу.
example2: You may abstain from voting if you have a conflict of interest.

en_definition: to give a task or responsibility to another person
example1: The project leader delegated tasks to each team member.
example1_ru: Руководитель проекта делегировал задачи каждому члену команды.
example2: Learning to delegate effectively saves time in group work.

en_definition: to work closely with another person or group and share information
example1: The student representative liaises with the faculty on scheduling issues.
example1_ru: Представитель студентов поддерживает связь с преподавателями по вопросам расписания.
example2: Our office liaises with the international students' association.

en_definition: to organize different people or things so they work well together
example1: She coordinates the schedule for all the lab sessions.
example1_ru: Она координирует расписание всех лабораторных занятий.
example2: The teams need to coordinate closely to finish the project on time.

en_definition: to watch over and make sure work is done correctly
example1: The supervisor oversees all the students' laboratory experiments.
example1_ru: Руководитель курирует все лабораторные эксперименты студентов.
example2: A committee oversees admissions decisions each year.

en_definition: to manage or organize the running of something
example1: The exam was administered by the registrar's office.
example1_ru: Экзамен проводился деканатом.
example2: The department administers scholarships for new students.

en_definition: a process made simpler and more efficient
example1: The new streamlined process cut registration time in half.
example1_ru: Новый упрощённый процесс сократил время регистрации вдвое.
example2: A streamlined process reduces unnecessary paperwork.

en_definition: the time it takes to complete a task or process a request
example1: The turnaround time for grading essays is usually two weeks.
example1_ru: Время проверки эссе обычно составляет две недели.
example2: The office has improved its turnaround time for visa letters.

en_definition: an accumulation of unfinished work or unprocessed requests
example1: There is a backlog of applications waiting to be reviewed.
example1_ru: Скопилась очередь необработанных заявок, ожидающих рассмотрения.
example2: The office is working through a large backlog after the holidays.
